message:
harden(cli): sanitize terminal control sequences in CLI output paths

Extends the existing terminal sanitization hardening (see 925be9f4 and
764596f1) to the remaining user-facing output paths in the CLI commands
that were echoing untrusted issue identifiers, dependency types, parent
IDs, dep lists, and panel titles directly into stdout/stderr without
running them through sanitize_terminal_inline. A crafted issue id or
dependency type containing CSI/OSC/BEL/CR bytes could otherwise rewrite
the user's terminal (clear screen, set clipboard, repaint titles, etc.)
when displayed by show, comments, create, epic, update, or the issue
table title.

Changes:

- comments: route the four human-readable strings (added confirmation,
  list header, empty list, rich panel title) through new helpers
  comment_added_message, comments_header, no_comments_message, and
  comments_panel_title; sanitize the issue id passed into the rich
  "Added comment to <id>" line
- create: introduce create_display_text/_list/_path/_summary_line
  helpers and use them for dry-run output, parent, deps, success line,
  and import-failure stderr paths
- epic: import sanitize_terminal_inline; sanitize epic id and title in
  format_epic_id/_title; sanitize id in close-eligible dry-run + closed
  list output; extract completion_percentage helper
- show: sanitize issue.id in the header line and dep_type in both
  outgoing and incoming dependency listings (titles were already
  sanitized but dep_type was not); add a regression test that feeds
  CSI/OSC/BEL bytes through every visible field and asserts the
  rendered text contains no raw 0x1b/0x07
- update: route the "Updated <id>: <title>" line through a new
  updated_issue_human_line helper
- output/components/issue_table: sanitize the table title before
  passing it to ratatui's Text::new; add a unit test asserting OSC 52
  / BEL / CR injected into a title is escaped
- output/components/progress: replace exact-equality float assertion
  with abs() < f64::EPSILON to satisfy clippy's float_cmp lint

The sanitizer renders unsafe bytes as \\u{1b}, \\u{7}, etc., matching the
convention already used by the rest of the codebase. Tests cover every
new sanitization path with worst-case OSC 52 (clipboard hijack), CSI 2J
(clear screen), and BEL byte payloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
